title: Sertula alba
taxonomy: Sertula alba is classified within the family Rosaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the rose family. This family includes a wide range of plants such as roses, apples, pears, and plums. Sertula alba is further classified as a member of the subfamily Rosoideae, which includes plants with pinnate or deeply lobed leaves. The genus Sertula is a relatively small one, with Sertula alba being one of the few species within it. This plant is native to the mountainous regions of Central and Southern Europe, and parts of Western Asia.

side_effects: Sertula alba, also known as the snow-in-summer, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine, but it may cause side effects due to its potential toxicity. intro: Sertula alba, also known as the white cockle or white burnet, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its white or pinkish flowers and fern-like foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infections. Sertula alba is rich in bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
